steps.title: Identify the cause-and-effect signal
steps.content: The passage moves from a concerning discovery—the tower was rotating in a concerning way—to an official response of closing the area, signaling that one event caused the other.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content: ”As a result,” directly marks cause-and-effect between the preceding discovery and the officials' action. The other three choices create parallel, illustrative, or comparative relationships rather than causation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
